description: CPC may find the filed ITR defective — incomplete, incorrect, or inconsistent. A defect notice u/s 139(9) is issued electronically. Taxpayer must rectify within 15 days (extendable). If not rectified, return is treated as invalid.
timeline: Rectify within 15 days of notice (extension possible on request to AO)

commonDefects.description: Required schedules not filled (e.g., Schedule S for salary income declared but Schedule S blank).
commonDefects.resolution: Re-file ITR with all mandatory schedules completed.

commonDefects.description: Filer used ITR-1 but has capital gains, or ITR-4 but owns 3+ properties.
commonDefects.resolution: File fresh return in correct form. Defective return treated as invalid.

commonDefects.description: Income declared lower than TDS credit claimed. TDS credits exceed declared income significantly.
commonDefects.resolution: Rectify income figure or recheck TDS schedule entries.

commonDefects.defect: Tax payable but no payment challan
commonDefects.description: Computation shows tax payable but no advance tax or self-assessment tax payment challan entered.
commonDefects.resolution: Pay tax and enter challan in Schedule IT before resubmitting.

commonDefects.description: In ITR-3/5/6: balance sheet totals don't balance (assets ≠ liabilities + capital).
commonDefects.resolution: Correct balance sheet figures and resubmit.

steps.description: Notice sent to registered email and on e-filing portal under 'Pending Actions'. Download notice to understand specific defects.
Correct the identified defects in the ITR. For wrong form — prepare fresh return in correct form.
Go to e-filing portal → 'Respond to Defective Return Notice'. Submit corrected return linked to original acknowledgment number.
E-verify the response/revised return. Confirmation sent by email.

commonMistakes: Ignoring defect notice — return becomes invalid after 15 days
Filing revised return instead of responding to defect notice (different procedures)
Not addressing ALL defects listed in notice
consequences: Unresponded defect: ITR treated as never filed — late filing fee u/s 234F applies
Loss carry-forward forfeited if return invalidated
